Assembly: The assembly icPhyViri1.hap1.1 is based on 60x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. This genome has been assembled using PacBio and HiC data and phased. The result is two curated haplotypes. Chromosome X identified by identification of ancestral X BUSCO genes. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome X (10.9 - 30.9) are unknown.

Assembly: The assembly of Atherina boyeri (fAthBoy1) is based on 64X PacBio data and 144X Arima Hi-C data generated as part of the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr).The assembly process included the following steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. In total, 1 contig weas identified as contaminants (bacterial), totaling 28 Kb. Additionally, 234 regions totaling 29.5 Mb (with the largest being 2.7 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using oatk. Finally, the primary assembly was analyzed and manually improved using Pretext. During manual curation, 5 haplotypic regions were removed, totaling 5.1 Mb (with the largest being 1.5 Mb). Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size.

Assembly: The assembly csSphRube1.1 is based on 52x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using OATK. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Chromosome 21 and 22 are microchromosomes. Chromosome 20 is about 1/4th of the size of an autosome, which is similar to what Healey, A.L., et. al (https://doi.org/10.1038/s41477-022-01333-5) observed for potential U/V sex chromosomes in other Sphagnums.

Assembly: This project provides the alternate haplotype of the genome assembly of Sphagnum jensenii, a peat moss with common name Jensen's sphagnum. The sample was provided by Olena Meleshko & Kristian Hassel. The assembly is provided by EBP-Nor (https://www.ebpnor.org/). The assembly csSphJens2.1 is based on 30x PacBio HiFi data, 55x Arima Hi-C data and generated by the EBP-Nor project. The assembly was filtered for adapters with HiFiAdapterFilt., assembled with hifiasm together with Hi-C data to generate a pair of haplotype-resolved assemblies. K-mers from the two assemblies were used to filter Hi-C reads to get two sets of reads to scaffold the assemblies (reads used to scaffold hap1 did not contain k-mers unique to hap2). YaHS was used for scaffolding. FCS-adaptor was used to remove putative adapters, before putative contaminants were removed with FCS-GX. The chromosomes confirmed by Hi-C data have been named based on synteny to Sphagnum angustifolium (named as S. fallax). The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were identified with Oatk and added to hap1.

Assembly: The assembly ilEuxSegn1.hap2.1 is based on 36x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by Project Psyche (https://www.projectpsyche.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. This genome has been assembled using PacBio and HiC data and phased. The result is two curated haplotypes. Z chromosome identified based BUSCO gene painting with ancestral Merian elements (https://doi.org/10.1038/s41559-024-02329-4).A haplotypic inversion was observed in the region on chromosome 2 (6.7-18.6 Mbp), chromosome 27 (11.4-15.6 Mbp) and chromosome 28 (2.4-9.2 Mbp). Chromosomes B1 and B2 were identified by lack of homology with ancestral chromosomes.

Assembly: This project provides the genome assembly csSphTene7.1 of the haploid soft bogmoss, Sphagnum tenellum. The sample was provided by Olena Meleshko & Kristian Hassel, and the data generation and assembly were carried out by EBP-Nor (https://www.ebpnor.org/). csSphTene7.1 is based on 58x PacBio HiFi data and 160x Arima Hi-C data. The HiFi reads were filtered for adapters using HiFiAdapterFilt, and assembled with hifiasm, generating a primary assembly. The assembly was purged for haplotigs with purge_dups, before scaffolding with YaHS. FCS-adaptor was used to remove putative adapters, and putative contaminants were removed with FCS-GX. The chromosomes confirmed by Hi-C data are named according to size.

Assembly: The assembly of Euthalenessa oculata (wpEutOcul1.1) is based on 38X PacBio data and Arima Hi-C data generated as part of the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr). The assembly process included the following steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. In total, 51 contigs were identified as contaminants (bacterial, archaeal, or viral), totaling 1.9 Mb (with the largest being 0.25 Mb). Additionally, 823 regions totaling 192 Mb (with the largest being 6.69 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using OATK. Finally, the primary assembly was analyzed and manually improved using Pretext. During manual curation, 45 haplotypic regions were removed, totaling 42.3 Mb (with the largest being 3.75 Mb). Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size. Chromosome 9 contains several unlocalized contigs, with the largest being 7.7 Mb. It may correpond to the end of the chromosome, but contigs are hard to organize in this region.

Taxonomy_x: Eukaryota; Viridiplantae; Streptophyta; Embryophyta; Bryophyta; Sphagnophytina; Sphagnopsida; Sphagnales; Sphagnaceae; Sphagnum
Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Viridiplantae; Streptophyta; Embryophyta; Bryophyta; Sphagnophytina; Sphagnopsida; Sphagnales; Sphagnaceae; Sphagnum
Assembly: This project provides the genome assembly of Sphagnum jensenii, a peat moss with common name Jensen's sphagnum. The sample was provided by Olena Meleshko & Kristian Hassel. The assembly is provided by EBP-Nor (https://www.ebpnor.org/). The assembly csSphJens1.h2.1 is based on 32x PacBio HiFi data, 55x Arima Hi-C data and generated by the EBP-Nor project. The assembly was filtered for adapters with HiFiAdapterFilt., assembled with hifiasm together with Hi-C data to generate a pair of haplotype-resolved assemblies. K-mers from the two assemblies were used to filter Hi-C reads to get two sets of reads to scaffold the assemblies (reads used to scaffold hap1 did not contain k-mers unique to hap2). YaHS was used for scaffolding. FCS-adaptor was used to remove putative adapters, before putative contaminants were removed with FCS-GX. The chromosomes confirmed by Hi-C data have been named based on synteny to Sphagnum angustifolium (named as S. fallax). The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were identified with Oatk and added to hap1.

Taxonomy_x: Eukaryota; Viridiplantae; Streptophyta; Embryophyta; Bryophyta; Sphagnophytina; Sphagnopsida; Sphagnales; Sphagnaceae; Sphagnum

Assembly: The assembly csSphTene1.2 is based on 41x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. the exact order of the contigs on the telomeric region of chromosome 18 (0-2.6 Mbp) is unknown.

Assembly: The assembly eaMarGlac1.2 is based on 49x PacBio data, 10X Genomics Chromium data, and Arima Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project (https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with HiCanu, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, short-read polishing using FreeBayes-called variants from 10X Genomics Chromium reads aligned with LongRanger, and Hi-C based scaffolding with SALSA2. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHifi. Finally, the assembly was analysed and manually improved using gEVAL. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.

Taxonomy_x: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Ecdysozoa; Arthropoda; Hexapoda; Insecta; Pterygota; Neoptera; Endopterygota; Coleoptera; Polyphaga; Elateriformia; Elateroidea; Cantharidae; Cantharinae; Cantharis
Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Ecdysozoa; Arthropoda; Hexapoda; Insecta; Pterygota; Neoptera; Endopterygota; Coleoptera; Polyphaga; Elateriformia; Elateroidea; Cantharidae; Cantharinae; Cantharis
Assembly: The assembly icCanPall1.hap1.1 is based on 45x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Chromosome X was identified by copy number in the diploid assembly. The species appears to be XO. The following regions of the assembly are of uncertain order and orientation: Chromosome 2 from ~24.8-43.6 Mbp, Chromosome 4 ~17.2-31.3 Mbp, Chromosome 5 ~17.9-26.9 Mbp and Chromosome 6 ~14.8-17.1 Mbp.
